mnemonics.title: DEADLOCK CONDITIONS
mnemonics.content: MEMO: MUTUAL EXCLUSION, HOLD AND WAIT, NO PREEMPTION, CIRCULAR WAIT - REMEMBER AS 'MHCN'

acronyms.usage: Remote kernel debugging using gdb over serial/Ethernet connection
Built-in Linux kernel tracer for function call analysis
Tool to intercept and log system calls made by a process
Virtual filesystem exposing kernel internals for debugging
Hardware and software event profiling tool
Kernel logging function similar to printf

visualTips.visualization: DRAW A FLOWCHART: ERROR DETECTED -> PANIC FUNCTION CALLED -> SAVE CRASH DUMP -> DISPLAY ERROR MESSAGE -> HALT SYSTEM -> RED BOOT FOR RECOVERY
DRAW FOUR INTERLOCKED CIRCLES IN A CHAIN: PROCESS A WAITS FOR B -> PROCESS B WAITS FOR C -> PROCESS C WAITS FOR A -> PROCESS A HOLDS RESOURCE 1, WANTS RESOURCE 2
DRAW A PYRAMID: BOTTOM (LOW-LEVEL) = KGDB/KDB, MIDDLE = PRINTK/DEBUGFS, TOP (HIGH-LEVEL) = SYSTEMTAP/PERF, APEX = CRASH DUMP ANALYSIS

analogies.analogy: DEBUGGING THE KERNEL IS LIKE REPAIRING THE ENGINE WHILE THE CAR IS RUNNING, WHERE APPLICATION DEBUGGING IS LIKE FIXING THE RADIO WITH THE ENGINE OFF.
TWO PEOPLE TRYING TO USE THE SAME PEN TO SIGN A DOCUMENT SIMULTANEOUSLY - WHOEVER GRABS IT FIRST DETERMINES THE OUTCOME, LEADING TO UNPREDICTABLE RESULTS.
TWO DRIVERS AT AN INTERSECTION EACH WAITING FOR THE OTHER TO GO - ONE HAS THE NORTH-SOUTH ROAD, THE OTHER HAS THE EAST-WEST ROAD, AND NEITHER CAN PROCEED.
PRINTK IS LIKE A BLACK BOX RECORDER IN AN AIRPLANE - IT CONTINUOUSLY LOGS MESSAGES SO AFTER A CRASH, INVESTIGATORS CAN RECONSTRUCT WHAT HAPPENED.
